headline: A US export-control directive forcing Anthropic to disable Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 dominated the cycle as inference-speed breakthroughs piled up alongside.
body: Across the recommended channels, the Claude Fable 5 withdrawal is the lead thread: Better Stack reports the US government ordered Anthropic to disable both Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for all customers immediately [7], Cole Medin ties the action to a claimed jailbreak and recaps benchmarks he ran before the pull [4], Cyril Imhof frames the EU-access fallout [6], and IBM Technology's podcast situates the release and its tier routing system alongside Apple's WWDC 2026 plan to use NVIDIA cloud AI over on-device models [9]. A parallel thread is raw inference throughput: Xiaomi and TileRT's 1-trillion-parameter MiMo V2.5 Pro UltraSpeed hits 3,000+ tokens per second via FP4 quantization, DFlash speculative decoding, and a persistent GPU kernel [3], while Google DeepMind's DiffusionGemma applies diffusion techniques to text for 1,000+ tokens per second on H100s [5]. On tooling, IBM Technology covers AI pair programming and MCP-driven design-system agents [0][2], and Better Stack profiles react-call and CopilotKit's generative UI [1][8]. Outside the inner circle, AICodeKing tested OpenRouter's Fusion judge-model system and found it short of Fable-level claims [10], while JavaScript Mastery and Chase AI weigh code-generation trust and Fable 5 agentic setups [11][13].
